title: Can't find out How to Add the Sales CRM to my Pro Account?
excerpt:
Hello, I am on the Pro account but can’t find the Sales CRM…only the basic one when looking in the template area? 
Thanks, 
Aaron

posts.author: aaronadamson (Aaron Adamson)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello, I am on the Pro account but can’t find the Sales CRM…only the basic one when looking in the template area?

Thanks,
Aaron

posts.author: BiancaT (Bianca Taylor)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey Aaron! Is this the template you’re looking for?

Screen Shot 2023-01-20 at 10.47.40 am510×576 36.5 KB

It should be available within your Pro plan. That being said, if you haven’t purchased the Sales CRM product, this particular template will not be available:

Screen Shot 2023-01-20 at 10.48.24 am514×616 94.7 KB

Let me know if this clears anything up!

posts.author: anon29275264 (anon29275264)
posts.cookedHtml: How do you actually purchase the CRM (or another) product? That’s the question I keep encountering, where is it at to purchase? who can make the purchase? etc.

posts.author: BiancaT (Bianca Taylor)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @anon29275264 - great question!

I believe this is the article you might be looking for: monday products pricing and features

The article details how you can purchase a product from a trial account, or purchase additional products on an existing account. To confirm, with any purchases on monday.com the admin of the account is responsible.

I hope this helps!

posts.author: benbloom (Benjamin Bloom)
posts.cookedHtml:
I came here because of the same issue as @anon29275264
I have the same issue, the instructions talk about a “grid icon on the bottom left corner of your screen (right above your profile picture)” As you can see, I do not have that:

I’d like to try the sales CRM features out, but I can’t figure out how without creating an entirely new account

posts.author: anon29275264 (anon29275264)
posts.cookedHtml:
My account when I go to purchase only offers “standard”, “pro”, and “enterprise”. There is no place to pick the other products. I looked at the article about pricing, and I can get to a page which looks like the one in the article showing the products on the left and the 5 tiers to purchase. However as soon as I login, it takes me to my account and the only place to purchase is from billing in the admin section.

image1739×837 76.5 KB

posts.author: anon29275264 (anon29275264)
posts.cookedHtml: This appears to be my case too - though I do have the grid, but no way to try or purchase the add on products.

posts.author: benbloom (Benjamin Bloom)
posts.cookedHtml: yeah. @BiancaT, I guess the question is: Is there a way to implement the new products from an account that predates the existence of the new plans? do we have legacy accounts or something? do we need to take steps to go from our current plans to the (same tier) plans that work with the “products”?

posts.author: BiancaT (Bianca Taylor)
posts.cookedHtml: https://monday.slack.com/archives/C02LF7L32SF/p1676602201046449

posts.author: BiancaT (Bianca Taylor)
posts.cookedHtml: Thank you @benbloom and @anon29275264 for this information. I have followed up with our products team about this and will respond with more information as soon as I hear back. Thanks in advance for your patience!

posts.author: sydneylynch (Sydney Lynch)
posts.cookedHtml:
I also encountered this and when I opened a ticket, this is the reply I got from the support team:

“You are currently using our classic infrastructure version. This means that monday products are not yet available for you. I do want to note that our development team is working on a solution to grant you the option to purchase products in the future. While there is no specific ETA for when this solution will be released, our team is working to make this available as soon as possible .”

and when I asked about what the pricing would be when it is available in our account I was told this:

"Great question - so the price for the add-on products depends on which plan tier you purchased for the Work OS, the number of seats/users that need access to the product and if you are paying monthly or yearly.

I’m happy to get this pricing information for you, however, I am not sure when you would be able to purchase products on your current account. The only way to get products is to open a brand new account with us."

posts.author: BiancaT (Bianca Taylor)
posts.cookedHtml:
Yes - I do believe that this is the reason for why you’re running into this roadblock. While I am still waiting on a concrete update from our developers, it appears that our older infrastructure isn’t yet able to supported the monday.com products I am afraid.

As for pricing, this article might offer some more detailed insight: monday products pricing and features

posts.author: BiancaT (Bianca Taylor)
posts.cookedHtml: Just following up on this one, as I have had confirmation that if your account is on our old infrastructure, regrettably you will not be able to access products just yet. I apologise for the setback here, however please know that this is something that our developers are working around the clock to implement. I will keep my eye on this and provide updates when they’re shared with me. I appreciate your understanding in advance

posts.author: aaronadamson (Aaron Adamson)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi Bianca, I moved my whole account to the new platform as I figured that I was boxed in with the old one! Thanks!

posts.author: BiancaT (Bianca Taylor)
posts.cookedHtml: I am sorry you felt this way @aaronadamson - this has been noted! That said, I am really glad you’re all sorted on our new infrastructure!

posts.author: mark.stout (Mark Stout)
posts.cookedHtml:
It seems the solution is to migrate your classic account to the new platform rather than wait for Monday.com to come up with a solution.

My account is renewing at the end of August 2023. Since this is a ready-made solution, how hard is it to migrate my existing data whicih doesm’t mirror the what Monday Sales CRM has.

  • Has anyone migrated and how was it done, I’m assuming exporting and importing?
  • What did you learn durting the process?
  • Any, “I should have done” tasks that would make the migration easier?

Also, if we open a new ac. count and migtrate, once we close our old account, can we re-use the old username?

Any assistance would be apreciated.

posts.author: mark.stout (Mark Stout)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Aaron,

Can you share your experience with migrating to and any “shoulda , coulda, woulda” that you discovered along the way? You’re right about being boxed in, given there’s no migration path.

posts.author: natrdorman (Natalie Dorman)
posts.cookedHtml: Has there been any update to this? I have been migrated to a new billing platform that allows me to purchase an entirely new, 3 user minimum package with Sales CRM but no option to add on to our existing Pro 15 user account. We haven’t even finished building our setup and the platform we’re on can’t add features?

posts.author: JSmyers (Jennifer Smyers)
posts.cookedHtml: Any update? This has been 10 months. We have a large Pro account for years and would love to use some of the new products. I think it’s pretty crappy you don’t take care of your long term customers!

posts.author: fifield21 (Carrie )
posts.cookedHtml: The Enterprise CRM is the most robust tool on the market. It’s so flexible and can literally do anything for just about any organization. I have built out different CRM’s for three separate companies, and with each build I was completely blown away how taking a use case, and being able to create something that supports that team, vs having the team cater to the CRM. If you ask me- if you are startup- monday’s CRM is the best option on the market.

posts.author: aaragon (Amanda A)
posts.cookedHtml: It has now almost been a year and there is no update. When will this be resolved? This seems like Monday.com does not care about keeping customers they have had for a long time. Why would this not be a priority?

posts.author: BiancaT (Bianca Taylor)
posts.cookedHtml: Checking in with Ron Kimhi
